sections.title: 一、完形填空
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，从所给的选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Once upon a time, a loving mother and her son, Tommy, lived in a small house. Tommy was a bright child who loved to learn new things. He always wanted to help his mother with the chores, and she happily [1] him to do so.

One night, Tommy's mother was feeling [2] and went to sleep early, so Tommy planned to prepare her favorite food for breakfast the next day, hoping she would feel better.

The next day, Tommy [3] went into the kitchen so he would not wake up his mother. He started by frying(煎) eggs. He carefully added salt and pepper, just in the way his mother liked. When the job was done, Tommy [4] to prepare the bread, place it in the toaster(烤面包机), and wait for it to pop up. Later, Tommy [5] some fresh oranges and made a glass of fruit juice.

When everything was ready, Tommy [6] the breakfast to his mother's room. He knocked on the door and walked in when he heard "Come in". His mother looked up to see Tommy with a smile on his face. "Good morning, Mommy!" Tommy said with [7] "I made you breakfast because I wanted to make you feel better."

His mother smiled at her little [8] "Oh, Tommy, this is the best breakfast I've ever had! You're such a kind kid with a [9] heart."

Tommy was happy to see that his mother enjoyed the breakfast he had prepared. Tommy [10] that it would be a lifetime memory for both of them.

questions.translation: 她高兴地鼓励他这样做。
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，母亲支持儿子的行为，encourage符合语境。

questions.translation: 母亲感到不舒服，早早睡了。
questions.explanation: 因为母亲生病了才需要儿子照顾，sick符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy安静地走进厨房。
questions.explanation: 为了不吵醒母亲，他必须安静地行动，quietly符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy继续准备面包。
questions.explanation: 这是做早餐的后续步骤，continued符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy选了一些新鲜橙子。
questions.explanation: 制作果汁需要选择橙子，chose符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy把早餐端到母亲房间。
questions.explanation: 端送食物用carry，carried符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy兴奋地说。
questions.explanation: 为母亲做早餐感到高兴，excitement符合语境。

questions.translation: 母亲对她的小厨师笑了。
questions.explanation: Tommy在做饭，cook符合语境。

questions.translation: 你有一颗善良的心。
questions.explanation: 形容心地善良，big heart表示慷慨或善良，big符合语境。

questions.translation: Tommy相信这将是终身的回忆。
questions.explanation: 对未来的一种信念，believed符合语境。

sections.title: 二、阅读理解
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，选择最佳答案。
sections.passage:
# ChoreMonster.make chores fun for kids!

ChoreMonster is a must-have app for families. It makes chores fun.
Kids get points by finishing their chores.
Kids get gifts like ice-cream, an hour of computer games, or a camping trip.
Parents plan their children's chores and the gifts they can get. ChoreMonster is best for kids from 4 to 14. If you need help in teaching your kids how to do the chores, download(下载) ChoreMonster today.

I've always been surprised since I downloaded the ChoreMonster app. My girls can't wait to help around the house!
--Jenny

The kids were excited about ChoreMonster. They woke up early and asked for more chores.
--Lisa

questions.translation: ChoreMonster能帮助孩子们做什么？
questions.explanation: 文章提到它让家务变得有趣，make housework interesting符合文意。

questions.translation: ChoreMonster最适合谁？
questions.explanation: 文章指出适合4到14岁的孩子，Jack是11岁，符合条件。

questions.prompt: What can we learn from the text?
questions.translation: 我们能从文中了解到什么？
questions.explanation: 文中明确提到Parents plan their children's chores and the gifts they can get。
options: Parents plan what gifts their kids can get.
Jenny's sons like ChoreMonster very much.
Kids can get money by using ChoreMonster.

sections.title: 三、阅读还原
sections.instruction: 阅读短文，从所给的选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项，其中有一个多余的选项。
sections.passage:
It is wrong that the whole family sit in front of the television while eating. [14] Instead, it only widens the gap between members of the family. So, it's better to give the TV a break while eating.

It's better to start the conversation right before dinner. [15] Doing those things together before dinner can make family members feel interested. As a result, there are no embarrassing(令人尴尬的) moments when people are actually eating.

Children aren't usually very good at sharing information. So don't ask them questions like "How was school today?" [16] Try to talk about the after-school activities that they might be taking part in. [17] If you have had a funny matter at work, share it. And it will [18] serve well if you share your childhood memories with your family. These interesting topics will make family members eat happily. 

In a word, it is always necessary to choose the right topics for the family dinner conversations.
wordbank: A. You should stop talking while eating.
B. Choose a nice TV program for your family.
C. They don't know how to reply to your questions.
D. Kids will feel excited to hear about what you did as a child.
E. The television does nothing to help make the family dinner conversations.
F. In that way, setting the table and doing other chores may become less boring.
G. Conversations during dinnertime should encourage family members to eat well.

questions.translation: 电视对家庭晚餐谈话毫无帮助。
questions.explanation: 上下文讨论电视对家庭交流的负面影响，E项符合。

questions.translation: 这样，摆放餐桌和做家务会变得不那么无聊。
questions.explanation: 前文提到晚餐前的准备活动，F项符合。

questions.translation: 他们不知道如何回答你的问题。
questions.explanation: 解释为什么不该问那种问题，C项符合。

questions.translation: 孩子们会很兴奋听到你小时候做过的事。
questions.explanation: 这是分享童年记忆的后续，D项符合。

questions.translation: 晚餐时的谈话应该鼓励家庭成员好好吃饭。
questions.explanation: 总结谈话的目的，G项符合。

sections.title: 四、任务型阅读
sections.instruction: 阅读下面短文，根据短文内容，完成下列各题。
sections.passage:
As we all know, doing housework is helpful for teenagers' development. Do teenagers in small villages do housework at home? How about the teenagers in big cities? We did a survey(调查) last month. We asked 4,000 teenagers in China. Half of them come from big cities. The others are from small villages. Let's look at the result. [HTML:

Housework Participation Survey

Teenagers in small villages
Teenagers in big cities
the number of teenagers
2000150010005000
take out
the rubbish
do the
dishes
sweep the
floor
fold the
clothes
make
the bed
housework
] We can find out that teenagers in small villages do more housework than those in big cities. Maybe it is because teenagers in small villages live a harder life or their parents are too busy to look after them. As a result, teenagers in small villages are more independent than those in big cities. We will give our advice to parents in big cities. We think parents should give their children some housework to do because it's a good chance(机会) for teenagers to learn how to take care of themselves. Besides, they should give their children more free time so that they can do what they love to do.

questions.prompt: Who does the underlined word "them" refer to?
questions.translation: 划线单词"them"指代谁？
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，指代4000名青少年。

questions.prompt: In the survey, how many more teenagers in small villages fold the clothes than those in big cities?
questions.translation: 调查中，农村折衣服的青少年比城市多多少？

questions.prompt: Why do teenagers in small villages do more housework than those in big cities?
questions.translation: 为什么农村青少年比城市青少年做更多家务？
questions.explanation: 文中提到他们生活更艰苦。
options: Their parents force them to do it.

questions.prompt: According to the passage, what can teenagers learn by doing housework?
questions.translation: 根据短文，青少年通过做家务能学到什么？
questions.explanation: 文中提到学习如何照顾自己。
options: How to take care of themselves.

questions.prompt: What do you usually do to help your parents at home?
questions.translation: 你通常在家帮父母做什么？
questions.explanation: 这是一个开放性问题，选择一个合理的家务活动。
